title: physalis lagascae
intro: Physalis lagascae, also known as the ground cherry or poha berry, is a type of plant that belongs to the nightshade family and is native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Physalis lagascae has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant's berries can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed fresh or dried to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
